basics.name: Matvey Sizov
basics.label: Senior Golang Developer
basics.summary: Backend engineer with 6+ years of commercial experience in microservices and distributed systems. I focus on reliable integrations (gRPC, Kafka, MQTT), performance optimization (SQL, indexing, async processing), and production hardening of high-load services. Based in Moscow, Russian citizen with work authorization in Russia, open to relocation and business trips. Preferred format: full-time, remote/hybrid/on-site.

work.name: ATOM
work.summary: Microservice platform with an MQTTv5 broker for cloud-to-vehicle communication, integrated with multiple internal systems over both synchronous and asynchronous channels.
highlights: Designed and maintained gRPC (sync) and Kafka (async) integrations with contract alignment and timeout/error handling.
Contributed to MQTTv5 broker development: access control, counterpart logic, background operations, and detailed metrics.
Implemented a per-client allowed-topic subscription system inside the broker.
Optimized performance by rewriting heavy SQL queries, tuning indexes, and improving critical business paths.
Moved selected flows to asynchronous Kafka processing, reducing latency and peak-time load.
Improved reliability by hardening against untrusted clients and standardizing failure handling across integrations.
Participated in PostgreSQL migrations, automated CI/CD, performed code reviews, and maintained technical documentation.
Stack: Golang, Mochi-MQTT, Redis, PostgreSQL, Kafka, mTLS, Grafana, Prometheus, Git.

work.name: SberTech
work.summary: Framework components based on Istio for Platform V: security mechanisms and workload management inside Kubernetes clusters, with policy and runtime control.
highlights: Built integrations with Platform V components and internal services over the Kubernetes REST API.
Designed framework components for security policies, workload management, metrics, and logging.
Built an automated test framework and CI pipeline from scratch in an isolated environment with Allure reporting.
Reintroduced unit testing practices and set up stable pipeline-based test execution.
Improved reliability through idempotent execution and protection against retry-related failures.
Performed code review, mentored School 21 interns, and maintained API/contract documentation.
Stack: Golang, Kubernetes, K8s Controller, Istio, PostgreSQL, Kafka, gRPC, Bitbucket.

education.area: Faculty of Information Technologies and Computer Security, Artificial Intelligence Technologies
